# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

parameters:
  sqlServerName:
    type: string
    default: StagingSqlServer
  sqlDatabaseName:
    type: string
    default: StagingSqlDatabase
  adminLogin:
    type: string
    default: sqladmin
  adminPassword:
    type: secureString
    default: 'AzureSQL2024!'
  developmentIps:
    type: array
    default:
      - '192.168.1.100'
      - '192.168.1.200'

resources:
  sqlServer:
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    name: StagingSqlServer
    location: West US
    properties:
      administratorLogin: ${adminLogin}
      administratorLoginPassword: ${adminPassword}

  sqlDatabase:
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases
    name: StagingSqlDatabase
    location: West US
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      serverId: ${sqlServer.name}
      requestedServiceObjectiveName: S1

  sqlFirewallRule:
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    name: AllowAllAzureIPs
    location: West US
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      resourceType: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets
      serverId: ${sqlServer.name}
      startIpAddress: 0.0.0.0
      endIpAddress: 255.255.255.255

  sqlFirewallRuleDevIPs:
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    name: AllowDevelopmentIPs
    location: West US
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      resourceType: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets
      serverId: ${sqlServer.name}
      startIpAddress: '${developmentIps[0]}'
      endIpAddress: '${developmentIps[1]}'

outputs:
  sqlDatabaseName: ${sqlDatabase.name}
  sqlServerName: ${sqlServer.name}
